problem.title: Binary Tree Longest Consecutive Sequence
problem.description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the length of the longest consecutive sequence path.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – DFS
solutions.description: Perform a DFS passing the current consecutive length down to each child: extend the length by 1 when the child's value is exactly one more than the parent's, otherwise reset to 1. The global maximum is updated at every node visit rather than only at leaves. The key insight is that the consecutive run is a path property that can be tracked purely top-down without needing a separate global helper.
